title: Oroko
content:
{{Top|ga|leg|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] in [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]]|the [[canon]] creature, the [[teal oroko]]}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:Oroko.jpg]] |name=''Oroko'' |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type=[[Transport/Legends|Transport]]{{CSWECite|II|424|Oroko}} |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=3[[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=[[Tractor beam/Legends|Tractor beam]] |complement=1 [[Unidentified shuttle (Oroko)|shuttle]] |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles= |affiliation= |navy= |fleet= |owners= |captains=[[Valius Ying]] |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|That's the ''Oroko''. Valius Ying, captain. I know these guys. Gangsters and pirates—and for the moment, bounty hunters.|Marn Hierogryph|}} The '''''Oroko''''' was a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]] that served as a mobile base for the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Valius Ying]]'s [[Valius Ying's gang|gang]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Operating out of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Ying and the ''Oroko'' hunted fugitive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] and accomplice [[Marn Hierogryph]] in [[3964 BBY]], after Carrick was accused of [[Padawan Massacre|murdering]] four classmates. The ''Oroko'' found its quarry in the [[Taris asteroid belt]] and captured them in the [[junk hauler]] [[The Last Resort (starship)|''Last Resort'']] with its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]. After Hierogryph struck a deal for his freedom, Ying brought the ''Oroko'' to Taris and left to turn Carrick over to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Meanwhile, Hierogryph bribed the ''Oroko''{{'s}} [[Unidentified first mate|first mate]] to drop ''The Last Resort'' off in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], allowing for Carrick's rescue. ==Description== {{Quote|Someone's shooting at us!''"
"''No—it's a tractor beam!|Marn Hierogryph and Zayne Carrick, as the ''Oroko'' seizes their ship|}} A massive [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''Oroko'' was sizable and accommodating enough to support a group of people as a mobile base. The ''Oroko'' had three forward-facing [[Engine/Legends|engines]] in front of a large, spherical frame and was equipped with a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]. The ship had a spacious [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], which was able to hold a [[junk hauler]] and a [[Unidentified shuttle (Oroko)|shuttle]] at the same [[Time/Legends|time]]. Largely a dirty brown [[Color/Legends|color]], the ''Oroko'' had four vertical [[orange]] stripes above its three engines. ==History== {{Quote|I thought you were going to leave with the ''Oroko!''"
"Gryph bribed the first mate to release the ''Last Resort'' in orbit—and Elbee still had schematics of your Jedi Tower.|Zayne Carrick and Jarael, after the former's rescue|[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]]}} The ''Oroko'' was owned by [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Valius Ying]], who used the vessel as a mobile base for his [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Valius Ying's gang|gang]] in the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Not normally a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], Ying, in [[3964 BBY]], joined the hunt for fugitive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]], accused of [[Padawan Massacre|murdering]] four classmates on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], and an accomplice, local [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] hood—and an old acquaintance of Ying's—[[Marn Hierogryph]]. Searching the [[Taris system/Legends|Taris system]] in the ''Oroko'', Ying found Carrick and Hierogryph aboard the junk hauler [[The Last Resort (starship)|''Last Resort'']], hidden in the [[Taris asteroid belt]]. The ''Oroko'' brought ''The Last Resort'' aboard with its tractor beam. In the ship's hangar, Hierogryph negotiated a deal for his freedom, along with that of ''Last Resort'' owners [[Jarael]] and [[Gorman Vandrayk|Gorman "Camper" Vandrayk]], in exchange for the entry codes to the Snivvian's Taris storehouses. Meanwhile, Ying would take Carrick alone in the ''Oroko''{{'s}} [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] to the Taris surface and turn him over to the authorities. Afterward, the ''Oroko'' would let ''The Last Resort'' off in another [[Star system/Legends|system]]. After the ''Oroko'' arrived in Taris' vicinity, Ying and Carrick departed in the shuttle. Met by a huge throng of spectators, Ying [[Comlink/Legends|commed]] the ''Oroko'' when he landed to tell the ship's crew he had arrived safely and proceeded on to give Carrick to the five [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] of the Tarisian [[Jedi Tower]]. Discovering that the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Masters had actually [[Death/Legends|killed]] the Padawans and framed Carrick, Ying attempted to leave the room quickly but instead was killed by Jedi Master [[Lucien Draay]]. Meanwhile, Hierogryph, concerned for his friend Carrick's safety, bribed the ''Oroko''{{'s}} [[Unidentified first mate|first mate]] to release ''The Last Resort'' in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], allowing Jarael to break into the Tower and rescue Carrick. ==Commanders and crew== Valius Ying commanded the ''Oroko'', which served as a mobile base for a number of [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]] who worked for the Twi'lek pirate. The ''Oroko'' also had a first mate, who took over command of the ship while Ying was away. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Oroko'' made its first appearance in [[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]], illustrated by [[Travel Foreman]], and released on [[May 31]], [[2006]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-891/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-5|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #5}} ==Appearances== *[[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] {{1st}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Oroko |pl=Oroko }} [[Category:Pirate starships]] [[Category:Transports]]
